text: The defining moment Zimbabwean reform came 2008 after the disputed and violence riddled elections 2008 when the three main political parties entered into transitional Government National Unity and were tasked with the establishment new constitution which was ultimately adopted 2013 following protracted and turbulent process which began 2009. Some segments the civil society however argued that the concerned political parties had ‘captured the project and narrowed short-term struggle motivated the pursuit party political interests the expense the will the people and nation’s broad long-term interests’, and thereby subverted and/or negated the aspirations the people. against this background that the study therefore assesses the participation, role and significance the rural populace the drafting the 2013 Zimbabwean Constitution. The study traces the history reform efforts Zimbabwe, beginning with the colonial Lancaster House Constitution 1979, the protracted exercise 2009 2013 which gave birth the current Constitution. focuses the 2009-13 constitution making process case study. The study employs approach adopting both doctrinal and empirical research approaches. The study employed the doctrinal research approach provide for doctrinal analysis the relevant global, regional and domestic legislation and case law. The empirical research approach, through interviews, was used collect qualitative data from the general members the rural populace an

d key institutions such political parties and human rights organisations from three selected rural districts, namely Bulilima, Makonde and Mutasa. The study indicated that while significant number the rural populace participated the constitution making process, the legal environment which subsisted during the constitution making process did not allow for the unfettered flow information and ideas, direct result repressive legislation such AIPPA, Criminal Law (Codification and Reform) Act, Interception Act and POSA, among others similar laws, and well the deeply polarised political environment owing the nature the relationship between the ZANU PF-led government and the opposition political parties. The study further showed that the process was heavily dominated the political parties the Global Political Agreement and all the political parties wanted ensure the adoption constitution that best reflected their preferences and partisan views rather than the will the masses, making the 2013 Constitution elitist negotiated document, contrary the provisions Article the GPA which provided for the right Zimbabweans make constitution for themselves and themselves.

coalition promoting initiative expand Medicaid coverage Montana and big increase tobacco taxes help pay for has filed complaint charging its opponents with illegal campaign activities.
The complaint was filed Monday with the state Commissioner Political Practices Tara Veazey, initiative campaign manager for Healthy Montana, coalition health-care organizations and other groups that pushing Initiative 185, which would raise the state tax cigarettes pack.
Those activities include the failure report expenditures made the campaign against I-185, and the use automated telephone solicitations, commonly known “robocalls,” which are illegal Montana unless live operator first obtains permission deliver the recorded message.
The charges are aimed Montanans Against Tax Hikes, group based Helena, Decision Point Consulting, based Ohio, “and any other now unknown entity committee” that has financially supported the illegal activities.
Commissioner Political Practices Jeff Mangan said the complaint was filed Monday, but still has examined determine whether meets all the requirements accepted his office. said that decision should made Wednesday.
Veasey said interview Monday that the complaint won’t have any bearing I-185, which will appear the ballot November supporters manage collect verified signatures from least 25,468 registered voters statewide June 22.

She said the complaint was filed force dark-money groups publicly revealed, and let Montanans know who spending money influence elections.
“We just want make sure that’s being disclosed, early and often possible,” she said.
Besides raising state taxes cigarettes pack, I-185 would increase taxes smokeless tobacco percent and create new taxes vaping and products.
also would extend Montana’s expanded Medicaid program, which provides health care more than 94,000 low-income Montanans, past 2019.
Denowh, the former director the Montana Republican Party and president the Montana Group, political consulting firm based Helena, did not return calls seeking comment, but told Yellowstone Public Radio was still reviewing the complaint and had comment.
According the complaint, “several individuals” reported receiving robocalls around May offering them $100 take part focus group. Veazey says the complaint that when she called the number provided, her call was answered voicemail service for Decision Point Consulting.
“Clearly,” the complaint says, “the hiring out state company produce robocall expenditure,” creating and conducting focus group meeting. But Decision Point Consulting has not filed C-2, spending disclosure statement with the state.
“If MATH paid for this poll conducted, should have been reported the C-6 disclosure report that was due May 21. MATH filed form C-6 June but this report failed include any the expenditures,” the complaint said.

April 2018, Adam Armoush, 21-year-old Israeli Arab living Berlin and his German friend Moroccan origin went through street Prenzlauer Berg taking selfies while wearing kippahs. According Armoush, was trying win argument with Jewish friends his Israel proving that could walk through Berlin wearing Jewish skullcap. one point, argument with three Arab-speaking young men started, which was followed violent attack. Armoush managed document the violence with his cell phone and later allowed the video shared Facebook, from where the video went viral. The video shows the main attacker, later identified 19-year-old Palestinian from refugee camp Syria who had come Germany war refugee, beat Armoush repeatedly with his belt. During the onslaught, the attacker shouted the word "Yahudi", the Arabic word for Jew, before one his companions intervened and dragged him away. According Armoush, the main attacker had also tried hurt him with glass bottle. consequence the beating, Armoush suffered minor injuries. Before turning himself police, the main attacker contacted the Palestinian student community Berlin and appeared short video that was distributed social media. They denied was hostile towards Jews that his actions had been motivated anti-Semitism.
The main attacker turned himself accompanied his lawyer the day after the incident. arrest warrant against him was executed.

Several politicians, among them chancellor Angela Merkel and foreign minister Heiko Maas condemned the attack. the Israeli television, Merkel warned persistent hatred Jews Germany. She spoke "different form anti-Semitism" coming from people Arab descent. After this and other incidents, the President the Central Council Jews Germany, Josef Schuster, warned Jewish men "not show themselves openly with kippah the metropolitan milieu Germany".
Under the slogan "Berlin Wears Kippah" the Jewish community Berlin called all citizens wear the kippah April solidarity with the victims.
"Anti-Semitic crimes increase dramatically Germany". Jewish Telegraphic Agency. August 2018. Retrieved August 2018.
Eddy, Melissa; Curry, Andrew (18 April 2018). "Fury Germany Rap Duo With Anti-Jewish Lyrics Gets Award". New York Times. Retrieved April 2018.
"'Jewish Man' Attacked Berlin Admits He's Israeli-Arab Who Didn't Believe Germany Was anti-Semitic". Haaretz. DPA, AP. April 2018.
